ontology.name: NAUClassSearchOntology
ontology.description: Grounded ontology for the NAU Class Search Data Agent. Internal RDF identifiers are kept stable so the ontology playground preserves the graph connections, while entity display titles use underscores and relationship labels read as natural language. The model separates Course from Course_Offering, models Course_Offering as subject plus catalog number, places prerequisites and requisites on Course_Offering, separates Term from Session, models multiple Sessions per Term with week lengths, connects Buildings to Campus and Campus to College, and models Class_Section enrollment through Academic_Policy and Reserve_Capacity_Rule.

entityTypes.name: Course
entityTypes.description: Catalog-level academic course concept grounded in course catalog data. A Course represents the stable academic idea, including title, units, career, level, and long catalog description text. Specific offerable variants, subject/catalog-number combinations, course_offer_number, and requisite logic are modeled through Course_Offering.

properties.name: course_id
properties.description: PeopleSoft COURSE_ID or durable course identifier.

properties.name: course_description
properties.description: Long catalog description text used for text-based AI search and retrieval.

properties.name: academic_career
properties.description: Academic career such as UGRD or GRAD.

properties.name: academic_level
properties.description: Academic level such as undergraduate or graduate.

entityTypes.name: Course_Offering
entityTypes.description: Offerable course concept grounded in the PeopleSoft course_id + course_offer_number pattern. A Course_Offering is made up of a Subject and catalog_number, belongs to a parent Course, can be scheduled as Class_Sections, and is the correct place to attach prerequisite, co-requisite, placement, equivalency, and recommended preparation rules.

properties.name: course_offering_key
properties.description: Business identifier for a Course_Offering, normally course_id + course_offer_number.

properties.name: course_id
properties.description: PeopleSoft COURSE_ID inherited from the parent Course.

properties.name: course_offer_number
properties.description: PeopleSoft COURSE_OFFER_NUMBER. This belongs to Course_Offering, not the generic Course concept.

properties.name: subject_code
properties.description: Subject code that forms the first part of the class offering, such as CS, BIO, MATH, PSY, or EE.

properties.name: catalog_number
properties.description: Catalog number that forms the second part of the class offering, such as 101, 238, or 310.

properties.name: course_code
properties.description: Combined subject + catalog number display code such as CS 310 or MATH 238.

properties.name: offering_status
properties.description: Whether the Course_Offering is active, inactive, or otherwise available to schedule.

entityTypes.name: Subject
entityTypes.description: Academic subject code such as CS, BIO, MATH, PSY, or EE. A Subject is mapped to an Academic_Organization and College so class search can reason from subject area to academic ownership.

properties.name: subject_code
properties.description: Subject code, such as CS, BIO, MATH, PSY, or EE.

entityTypes.name: College
entityTypes.description: College-level academic grouping. A College can contain multiple Academic_Organizations and can own or be associated with Subjects.

entityTypes.name: Class_Section
entityTypes.description: Scheduled class section grounded in class scheduling data. A Class_Section is the student-facing scheduled instance of a Course_Offering in a specific Session, with enrollment, capacity, status, delivery mode, class number, multiple Meeting_Patterns, instructors, Reserve_Capacity_Rules, and Academic_Policy controls for add, drop, and waitlist timing.

properties.name: class_section_business_key
properties.description: Composite business key for a section, generally course_id + course_offer_number + term_id + session_id + class_section_id.

properties.name: delivery_mode
properties.description: Delivery mode such as in-person, online, hybrid, or blended.

properties.name: enrollment_status
properties.description: Enrollment status such as open, closed, cancelled, waitlist, or tentatively scheduled.

properties.name: students_on_waitlist
properties.description: Current number of students on the waitlist.

properties.name: class_length_in_weeks
properties.description: Length of the Class_Section in weeks when calculated or available.

properties.name: dynamically_dated
properties.description: Whether the Class_Section uses dynamically dated add, drop, or enrollment dates.

entityTypes.name: Term
entityTypes.description: Academic term such as Fall, Spring, or Summer. A Term is measured in number of weeks and can contain multiple Sessions. For example, a Summer Term may be 12 weeks while it contains multiple 5-week summer sessions.

properties.name: term_description
properties.description: Term description such as Fall 2026, Spring 2027, or Summer 2027.

properties.name: term_length_in_weeks
properties.description: Length of the Term in weeks. Example: a Summer Term may be 12 weeks.

entityTypes.name: Session
entityTypes.description: Session within a Term, such as a 12-week summer session, 5-week summer session 1, 5-week summer session 2, or 5-week summer session 3. Each Session is measured in number of weeks and can contain multiple Class_Sections.

properties.name: session_key
properties.description: Unique Session key, normally term_id + session_id.

properties.name: session_id
properties.description: Session code such as 12W, 5W1, 5W2, 5W3, M16, N5A, N5B, N10, N12, or dynamically dated session code.

properties.name: session_description
properties.description: Session description such as Summer 5-week Session 1.

properties.name: session_length_in_weeks
properties.description: Length of the Session in weeks. Example: Summer sessions may be 5 weeks within a 12-week Summer Term.

entityTypes.name: Academic_Policy
entityTypes.description: Academic policy governing Class_Section enrollment timing and rules, including open enrollment, add/drop periods, waitlist policy, withdrawal deadlines, and related session or section-specific controls.

properties.name: academic_policy_id
properties.description: Identifier for an enrollment date control rule.

properties.name: open_enrollment_end_date
properties.description: Date when open enrollment closes or changes.

properties.name: add_start_date
properties.description: Date when students may begin adding a Class_Section.

properties.name: add_end_date
properties.description: Last date to add a Class_Section.

properties.name: drop_start_date
properties.description: Date when students may begin dropping a Class_Section.

properties.name: drop_end_date
properties.description: Last date to drop a Class_Section without the relevant penalty.

properties.name: policy_source
properties.description: Whether the date rule is derived from term, session, class section, dynamic dating, or another registrar rule source.

entityTypes.name: Instructor
entityTypes.description: Professor or instructor assigned to teach a Class_Section. Uses EMPLID or another person identifier as the stable natural key and instructor_name as the display value.

properties.name: emplid
properties.description: Employee or person identifier for the instructor.

properties.name: instructor_role
properties.description: Instructor role such as primary instructor, secondary instructor, or teaching assistant.

entityTypes.name: Academic_Organization
entityTypes.description: Academic owner such as college, division, department, school, or academic organization. Supports questions by organization, subject ownership, class ownership, and program ownership.

properties.name: academic_org_sk
properties.description: Surrogate or business key for the academic organization.

properties.name: organization_type
properties.description: Organization type such as college, division, department, school, or program-owning unit.

entityTypes.name: Meeting_Pattern
entityTypes.description: Meeting days, meeting times, and location pattern for a Class_Section. A Class_Section can have multiple Meeting_Patterns for lecture, lab, fieldwork, or other components.

properties.name: meeting_days
properties.description: Meeting days such as MWF, TuTh, or Online.

entityTypes.name: Room
entityTypes.description: Physical room or facility where a Meeting_Pattern occurs. A Facility_Room is located in a Building.

entityTypes.name: Building
entityTypes.description: Campus building that contains one or more Facility_Rooms. A Building is located on a Campus.

entityTypes.name: Campus
entityTypes.description: Campus or location context for Buildings and Class_Sections, such as the Flagstaff Mountain Campus. A Campus belongs to a College in this ontology design.

entityTypes.name: Reserve_Capacity_Rule
entityTypes.description: Reserve capacity rule that allows eligible student populations to enroll in a Class_Section. A Class_Section can have multiple Reserve_Capacity_Rules with reserved seats, eligible populations, consent requirements, and enrollment counts.

properties.name: reserve_sk
properties.description: Surrogate key for the reserve capacity rule.

properties.name: reserve_start_date
properties.description: Date when the reserve capacity rule begins.

properties.name: reserve_group_description
properties.description: Description of the student group eligible for the reserved seats.

properties.name: eligible_population
properties.description: Population allowed to enroll through this reserve rule, such as plan, campus, cohort, career, or student group.

properties.name: enrollment_consent
properties.description: Enrollment consent requirement if the reserve rule requires permission.

properties.name: reserve_capacity
properties.description: Number of seats reserved for the eligible population.

properties.name: reserve_enrolled
properties.description: Number of students enrolled against the reserve capacity.

properties.name: reserve_open_seats
properties.description: Available seats under the reserve capacity rule.

entityTypes.name: Class_Attribute
entityTypes.description: Attribute attached to a Class_Section or Course_Offering, such as Liberal Studies, CCAT, component, lecture, lab, fieldwork, or other designations.

entityTypes.name: Requisite_Rule
entityTypes.description: Prerequisite, co-requisite, equivalency, placement, or recommended preparation rule. Requisite_Rules attach to Course_Offerings and point to prerequisite Course_Offerings, which avoids a direct Course-to-Course loop.

properties.name: requisite_rule_id
properties.description: Identifier for the prerequisite, co-requisite, equivalency, placement, or recommended preparation rule.

properties.name: requisite_type
properties.description: Type such as prerequisite, co-requisite, equivalency, placement, or recommended preparation.

properties.name: logical_operator
properties.description: Logical operator such as AND, OR, concurrent, minimum score, or minimum grade grouping.

entityTypes.name: Academic_Program
entityTypes.description: Degree, plan, certificate, or academic program that contains Academic_Requirements and may require Courses or Course_Offerings.

properties.name: catalog_code
properties.description: Catalog requirement code or catalog year when available.

entityTypes.name: Academic_Requirement
entityTypes.description: Requirement inside an Academic_Program, such as capstone, lab science, Liberal Studies, major requirement, or prerequisite-ready pathway. Requirements may be satisfied by Courses or Course_Offerings.

properties.name: requirement_type
properties.description: Requirement type such as capstone, lab science, Liberal Studies, prerequisite, or major requirement.

relationships.name: has
relationships.description: A catalog-level Course can have one or more Course_Offerings. This separates the stable course concept from PeopleSoft course_offer_number and offering-specific requisite rules.

relationships.name: of
relationships.description: A Course_Offering belongs to one parent Course.

relationships.name: has
relationships.description: A Course_Offering is made up of a Subject and catalog_number.

relationships.name: owned by
relationships.description: A Subject is mapped to the Academic_Organization that owns or administers that subject area.

relationships.name: owns
relationships.description: A College can contain multiple Academic_Organizations such as divisions, schools, or departments.

relationships.name: schedules
relationships.description: A Course_Offering can be scheduled as many Class_Sections across Terms and Sessions.

relationships.name: of
relationships.description: A Class_Section is the scheduled instance of one Course_Offering, joined by course_id and course_offer_number plus term, session, and section identifiers.

relationships.name: has
relationships.description: A Term can have multiple Sessions, and each Session is measured in number of weeks. For example, a 12-week Summer Term may contain 5-week summer sessions 1, 2, and 3.

relationships.name: in
relationships.description: A Session belongs to one Term.

relationships.name: in
relationships.description: A Class_Section is offered in one Session, and the Session belongs to a Term.

relationships.name: has
relationships.description: A Session can include many Class_Sections.

relationships.name: governed by
relationships.description: A Class_Section is controlled by an Academic_Policy for open enrollment, add/drop dates, waitlist dates, and withdrawal deadlines.

relationships.name: taught by
relationships.description: A Class_Section is taught by a Professor_Instructor.

relationships.name: teaches
relationships.description: A Professor_Instructor teaches one or more Class_Sections.

relationships.name: affiliated with
relationships.description: A Professor_Instructor may be affiliated with an Academic_Organization.

relationships.name: has
relationships.description: A Class_Section can have multiple Meeting_Patterns for lecture, lab, fieldwork, discussion, or other scheduled components.

relationships.name: occur in
relationships.description: A Meeting_Pattern occurs in a specific Facility_Room.

relationships.name: in
relationships.description: A Facility_Room is located in a Building.

relationships.name: on
relationships.description: A Building is located on a Campus.

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: A Campus belongs to a College.

relationships.name: has
relationships.description: A Class_Section can have multiple Reserve_Capacity_Rules that allow eligible student populations to enroll in the section.

relationships.name: applies to
relationships.description: A Reserve_Capacity_Rule allows students in an eligible population to enroll in a Class_Section when seats are reserved for them.

relationships.name: has
relationships.description: A Class_Section can carry Liberal Studies, CCAT, component, modality, lecture, lab, fieldwork, or other attributes.

relationships.name: has
relationships.description: A Course_Offering can have prerequisite, co-requisite, placement, equivalency, or recommended preparation rules that must be evaluated before a student takes a Class_Section of that offering.

relationships.name: applies to
relationships.description: The target Course_Offering the student wants to take. This is the offering whose Class_Sections are blocked or conditioned by the Requisite_Rule.

relationships.name: has
relationships.description: An Academic_Program has one or more Academic_Requirements.

relationships.name: satisfied by
relationships.description: An Academic_Requirement may be satisfied by one or more Courses.

relationships.name: owned by
relationships.description: An Academic_Program is owned by an Academic_Organization.

ontology.name: preventiveHealthcare — Wellbeing Check Ontology v0.6
ontology.description: D2C wellbeing-check ontology for U.S. working-age adults. v0.6: Purchase becomes the anchor for channel, payment, and product attribution. 15 entities, 17 relations. Entity numbering and ont:x / ont:y reflect the spatial layout from "Ontology 0.5 - Final - Corrections.png" (top-to-bottom, left-to-right).

entityTypes.name: ActionCompletion
entityTypes.description: A Member's completion of a recommended Action. Source indicates whether it was self-reported, system-detected, or verified.

entityTypes.name: EngagementMessage
entityTypes.description: A nudge, reminder, or coaching message sent to a Member, seeded by a WellbeingReport.

entityTypes.name: EmployerChannelPartner
entityTypes.description: Employer or affiliate through which a Purchase was acquired (sponsored fair, co-branded deal, etc.).

entityTypes.name: Action
entityTypes.description: A recommendation generated by a WellbeingReport (lifestyle change, follow-up booking, PCP referral, habit). Optionally targets a specific RiskFlag.

entityTypes.name: WellbeingReport
entityTypes.description: The Member-facing deliverable produced from a Purchase. Carries a composite score and delta vs. prior report.

entityTypes.name: Purchase
entityTypes.description: One Purchase = one wellbeing check delivery. Either a standalone package or a membership delivery. In v0.6 also anchors paidWith→PaymentMethod, attributedTo→EmployerChannelPartner, and forProduct→Product.

entityTypes.name: Member
entityTypes.description: The end consumer of the wellbeing check service. The Member is the subject of care whose wellbeing data is collected, scored, and tracked over time.

entityTypes.name: PaymentMethod
entityTypes.description: One row per card or bank account on file. Anchored to Purchase via paidWith.

entityTypes.name: RiskFlag
entityTypes.description: A clinical-risk indicator inferred from Observations (e.g., pre-diabetes, hypertension, depression risk). Has a lifecycle (opened → resolved).

entityTypes.name: BillingEvent
entityTypes.description: A billing attempt: charge, refund, or chargeback. reversesBillingEventId is set when kind = refund or chargeback (points to the original charge).

properties.name: reversesBillingEventId
properties.description: set when kind = refund or chargeback

entityTypes.name: Membership
entityTypes.description: A Member's enrollment in a recurring product. Transactional: each renewal creates a new row. Only the active row has isCurrent = true. Renewal count = row count per Member.

entityTypes.name: Product
entityTypes.description: Catalog SKU. The thing a Member buys (membership, package, or addon). Defines pricing and cadence.

entityTypes.name: Observation
entityTypes.description: A single data point produced by an Encounter — biomarker value, biometric reading, or questionnaire response. Coded with LOINC or SNOMED.

entityTypes.name: Encounter
entityTypes.description: A single touchpoint that produces data: Intake, Biometric measurement, or BloodDraw.

entityTypes.name: LabPartner
entityTypes.description: External laboratory that performs blood draws and biomarker analysis.

relationships.name: has
relationships.description: Member has Membership. Only one row per Member has isCurrent = true.

relationships.name: makes
relationships.description: Member makes Purchase.

relationships.name: includes
relationships.description: Purchase includes Encounter.

relationships.name: yields
relationships.description: Purchase yields WellbeingReport.

relationships.name: forProduct
relationships.description: Purchase forProduct Product. New in v0.6 — promotes the productId attribute to a first-class relation.

relationships.name: has
relationships.description: Purchase paidWith PaymentMethod.

relationships.name: attributedTo
relationships.description: Purchase attributedTo EmployerChannelPartner.

relationships.name: performedAt
relationships.description: Encounter performedAt LabPartner. Set for BloodDraw encounters only.

relationships.name: produces
relationships.description: Encounter produces Observation.

relationships.name: contributesTo
relationships.description: Observation contributesTo RiskFlag

relationships.name: lists
relationships.description: WellbeingReport lists RiskFlag.

relationships.name: recommends
relationships.description: WellbeingReport recommends Action.

relationships.name: seedsEngagement
relationships.description: WellbeingReport seedsEngagement EngagementMessage.

relationships.name: completedBy
relationships.description: Action completedBy ActionCompletion.

relationships.name: receives
relationships.description: Member receives EngagementMessage.

relationships.name: pays
relationships.description: BillingEvent pays Membership.

relationships.name: pays
relationships.description: BillingEvent pays Purchase.

entityTypes.name: Cabin

entityTypes.name: Ship

entityTypes.name: Guest

entityTypes.name: Booking

relationships.name: books

relationships.name: is assigned to

relationships.name: belongs to

relationships.name: works in

relationships.name: consumes

relationships.name: visits
relationships.description: Itinerary visits ports (1:N, with sequence number and times).

relationships.name: follows a

relationships.name: is operated by

ontology.name: Incident Management
ontology.description: Incident Management domain. It aligns with ITIL 4 best practices.

entityTypes.name: Problem
entityTypes.description: Known error or root cause for linking recurring incidents

entityTypes.name: ConfigurationItem
entityTypes.description: Underlying asset or component causing or affected by the incident – ITIL CMDB concept

entityTypes.name: Assignee
entityTypes.description: Support Analyst, Team, or Group responsible for resolution

entityTypes.name: Incident
entityTypes.description: Central concept. Unplanned interruption or reduction in service quality

entityTypes.name: Service
entityTypes.description: Business or IT Service being disrupted

entityTypes.name: User
entityTypes.description: Employee / Reporter. The person raising the incident

relationships.name: is supported by
relationships.description: Service is supported by Configuration Item

relationships.name: relates to
relationships.description: Incident is related to Problem

relationships.name: is assigned to
relationships.description: Incident is assigned to Assignee

relationships.name: impacts
relationships.description: Incident impacts Configuration Item

relationships.name: affects
relationships.description: Incident affects Service

entries.name: 재난 관리 기관 온톨로지
entries.description: 이 온톨로지는 응급 관리 및 출동 배정 시스템을 나타내며, 실제 응급 상황이 최초 신고부터 최종 해결까지 어떻게 처리되는지 보여줍니다. 신고자가 응급 상황을 보고하고, 배차 담당자가 상황을 평가하며, 대응 부대가 배치되고, 자원이 위치 간에 이동하며, 인시던트가 해결되고 종료될 때까지 이벤트가 기록되는 인시던트의 전체 수명 주기를 모델링합니다.
entries.author: Benito van Breugel

entityTypes.name: Incident_Resolution
entityTypes.description:
This represents how and why the incident was closed / solved.

Incident resolution is modeled explicitly through incident events and a resolution concept, allowing the ontology to capture not only when an incident ends, but also why and by whom it was resolved.

entityTypes.name: Incident_Event
entityTypes.description:
Represents all the time events happening on the incident.

Note that Incident_Event updates Incident_Classification

entityTypes.name: Fire_Station
entityTypes.description: A location where fire vehicles and firefighters are located and togther they form a response unit

entityTypes.name: Location
entityTypes.description: location or area type of incident

entityTypes.name: Incident_Classification
entityTypes.description: Specifies

entityTypes.name: Firefighter
entityTypes.description: Represents an individual responder.

ontology.name: Incident Management
ontology.description: Models the people, systems, and artifacts in an enterprise incident management workflow.

entityTypes.name: ChangeRequest
entityTypes.description: The governed deployment vehicle through which a resolution is released to a controlled environment. Provides the audit trail, approval, and scheduled window required by enterprise change-management processes.

entityTypes.name: Resolution
entityTypes.description: The fix or workaround applied to close the incident. May be a permanent code fix, a configuration change, a rollback to a previous version, or a temporary workaround pending a longer-term solution.

entityTypes.name: RootCause
entityTypes.description: The underlying defect or condition that produced the incident. Categorized by type (code, configuration, data, or infrastructure) to support trend analysis and prevention.

entityTypes.name: BusinessOwner
entityTypes.description: The accountable owner of the affected application or business process. Reports the real-world impact, signs off on the resolution, and is the final stakeholder informed when the incident is closed.

entityTypes.name: Tester
entityTypes.description: Validates the proposed fix in lower environments before it is released. Confirms that the resolution actually solves the original incident and does not introduce regressions.

entityTypes.name: BusinessAnalyst
entityTypes.description: Translates technical incident details into business impact and vice versa. Helps the technical team understand what business processes are affected and helps the business understand what is being done about it.

entityTypes.name: ScrumMaster
entityTypes.description: The team coordinator who runs the incident response process. Removes blockers, manages communication, runs the war room or bridge call, and ensures the right people are engaged at the right time.

entityTypes.name: SMEArchitect
entityTypes.description: A subject-matter expert or architect with deep domain or technical knowledge. Consulted when an incident requires authority beyond a single developer, such as cross-system design decisions or complex root-cause analysis.

entityTypes.name: Developer
entityTypes.description: The engineer assigned to investigate and fix the incident. Owns the technical debugging, code or configuration change, and works with other roles to drive the incident to resolution.

entityTypes.name: Environment
entityTypes.description: The deployment context where an incident occurs, such as Production, UAT, or Development. Distinguishing the environment is essential because the same defect can have very different business impact depending on where it surfaces.

entityTypes.name: Application
entityTypes.description: A software service or system that delivers business functionality. The application is the subject of an incident — what is broken, slow, or behaving unexpectedly.

entityTypes.name: Incident
entityTypes.description: An unplanned disruption or degradation of a service that requires investigation and resolution. Tracked by severity, status, and SLA target from the moment it is reported until it is closed.

relationships.name: affects
relationships.description: An incident affects a specific application — the system that is broken or degraded.

relationships.name: occursIn
relationships.description: Identifies which environment the incident surfaced in (Production, UAT, Dev).

relationships.name: assignedTo
relationships.description: When an incident requires deeper expertise, it escalates to a subject-matter expert or architect.

relationships.name: escalatesTo
relationships.description: When an incident requires deeper expertise, it escalates to a subject-matter expert or architect.

relationships.name: coordinatedBy
relationships.description: The scrum master who coordinates the response, runs the war room, and removes blockers.

relationships.name: consults
relationships.description: The developer consults the business analyst to understand business impact and clarify requirements.

relationships.name: notifies
relationships.description: The business analyst notifies the affected business owner about incident impact and progress.

relationships.name: identifies
relationships.description: The developer identifies the underlying defect or condition that caused the incident.

relationships.name: leadsTo
relationships.description: The root cause leads to a resolution - the fix or workaround that closes the incident.

relationships.name: validatedBy
relationships.description: The resolution is deployed to production through a governed change request, providing the audit trail.

relationships.name: deployedVia
relationships.description: The resolution is deployed to production through a governed change request, providing the audit trail.

ontology.name: Sales Analytics That Speaks Business—Using Fabric IQ Ontology
ontology.description: A Fabric IQ–powered sales ontology that turns transactional data into the language of the business — Customer places Order, Order generates Invoice, Return reduces Revenue — so leaders ask questions in plain business terms, not SQL.

entityTypes.name: Customer
entityTypes.description: A person or organization that purchases goods or services from the company. Holds an address, maintains a balance, and engages in orders, invoices, and returns.

entityTypes.name: Customer_Address
entityTypes.description: A physical or mailing location associated with a customer, used for billing and delivery.

entityTypes.name: Item
entityTypes.description: A product or service offered by the company. Can be ordered, stored in a warehouse, sold by a sales person, and transacted through a point of sale.

entityTypes.name: Warehouse
entityTypes.description: A physical facility where items are stored, orders are fulfilled, and returns are processed. Owned and operated by the company.

entityTypes.name: Department
entityTypes.description: An internal organizational unit that groups sales personnel, sits within a company, and contributes to overall revenue.

entityTypes.name: Sales_Person
entityTypes.description: An employee who handles customer orders, sells items, works within a department, and is evaluated through sales performance.

entityTypes.name: Company
entityTypes.description: The legal business entity that owns warehouses, operates points of sale, and employs sales personnel.

entityTypes.name: Order
entityTypes.description: A request placed by a customer to purchase one or more items. Fulfilled from a warehouse, handled by a sales person, recorded at a point of sale, and generates an invoice.

entityTypes.name: Invoice
entityTypes.description: A billing document generated from an order and issued to a customer. Records the amount payable and contributes to revenue.

entityTypes.name: Return
entityTypes.description: A transaction in which a customer sends back items from a prior order. Processed at a warehouse and reduces recognized revenue.

entityTypes.name: Customer_Balance
entityTypes.description: The current outstanding monetary amount owed by, or credit held for, a customer at a given point in time.

entityTypes.name: Sales_Performance
entityTypes.description: A measure of a sales person's effectiveness over a period, evaluated in terms of revenue achieved.

entityTypes.name: Revenue
entityTypes.description: The total monetary income recognized by the business, generated through invoices, departments, and points of sale, and reduced by returns.

entityTypes.name: Point_of_Sales
entityTypes.description: A location or terminal where sales transactions are completed and recorded. Operated by the company and generates revenue.

ontology.name: Learning Ontology
ontology.description: Canonical learning ontology derived from the reference model in clean_with_keys_and_attributes.owl. It defines business entities, explicit key attributes, required non-key attributes, and core relationships for a learning domain.

entityTypes.name: Person
entityTypes.description: A learner or worker participating in learning programs, assignments, completions, and skill tracking.

entityTypes.name: LearningProgram
entityTypes.description: A structured learning offering that can group curricula and coordinate mandatory or optional development activities.

entityTypes.name: Curriculum
entityTypes.description: A rules-based grouping of courses or learning items with an effective validity period and completion requirements.

entityTypes.name: Course
entityTypes.description: A formal course container with a delivery model and duration measured in hours.

entityTypes.name: LearningItem
entityTypes.description: An atomic learning resource such as a module, class, video, assessment, or external content item.

entityTypes.name: LearningAssignment
entityTypes.description: An assignment of a learning item to a person, including business reason and required completion dates.

entityTypes.name: LearningCompletion
entityTypes.description: A completion event for a learning item or assignment, including status, date, score, and optional attempt number.

properties.name: attemptNumber
properties.description: Optional non-key attribute for LearningCompletion representing retry count.

entityTypes.name: Skill
entityTypes.description: A capability, competency, or skill domain that can be developed by learning and associated with a person.

relationships.name: hasCurriculum
relationships.description: Links a learning program to a curriculum that belongs to it.

relationships.name: containsCourse
relationships.description: Links a curriculum to courses that satisfy its structure.

relationships.name: includesLearningItem
relationships.description: Links a course to one or more learning items that are delivered as part of the course.

relationships.name: assignedTo
relationships.description: Links a learning assignment to the person who must complete it.

relationships.name: assignsLearningItem
relationships.description: Links a learning assignment to the learning item being assigned.

relationships.name: resultsInCompletion
relationships.description: Links an assignment to the completion that records the result.

relationships.name: completedBy
relationships.description: Links a completion record to the person who completed the learning.

relationships.name: completesLearningItem
relationships.description: Links a completion record to the learning item that was completed.

relationships.name: hasSkill
relationships.description: Links a person to a skill they hold or have demonstrated.

relationships.name: teachesSkill
relationships.description: Links a learning item to a skill it develops.

ontology.name: Supply Chain Disruption & Risk Propagation
ontology.description: Models how a disruption to one supplier cascades through components and product lines, enabling AI to recommend mitigation before the impact reaches customers.

entityTypes.name: Supplier
entityTypes.description: External company that provides raw materials or components

properties.name: singleSourced
properties.description: True if no approved alternative exists

entityTypes.name: Component
entityTypes.description: A part, material, or sub-assembly sourced from one or more suppliers

properties.name: criticalityLevel
properties.description: Impact level if this component is unavailable

entityTypes.name: ProductLine
entityTypes.description: A group of finished products that share one or more common components

properties.name: annualRevenue
properties.description: Annual revenue attributed to this product line

entityTypes.name: DisruptionEvent
entityTypes.description: An event that interrupts or threatens normal supply from one or more suppliers

entityTypes.name: RiskAssessment
entityTypes.description: An analysis of the business impact when a disruption affects the supply chain

properties.name: revenueAtRisk
properties.description: Estimated revenue impacted if no action taken

properties.name: timeToImpactDays
properties.description: Days until production is affected if supply is not restored

entityTypes.name: MitigationAction
entityTypes.description: A concrete step taken to reduce or eliminate the impact of a supply disruption

properties.name: leadTimeSavedDays
properties.description: Days of disruption impact avoided by this action

entityTypes.name: AlternativeSupplier
entityTypes.description: A qualified backup supplier capable of substituting for a disrupted primary supplier

relationships.name: supplies
relationships.description: A supplier provides one or more components to the operation

relationships.name: usedIn
relationships.description: A component is incorporated into one or more product lines

relationships.name: affects
relationships.description: A disruption event impacts the supply capability of a supplier

relationships.name: triggers
relationships.description: A disruption event causes one or more risk assessments to be performed

relationships.name: recommends
relationships.description: A risk assessment drives specific mitigation actions

relationships.name: activates
relationships.description: A mitigation action may bring alternative suppliers into production

relationships.name: canReplace
relationships.description: An alternative supplier is qualified to substitute for a primary supplier

entityTypes.name: Reaction
entityTypes.description: An emoji response attached to a message

entityTypes.name: Thread
entityTypes.description: A scoped sub-conversation within a channel

entityTypes.name: Moderation_Action
entityTypes.description: A logged enforcement action (warn, mute, ban, kick)

entityTypes.name: Event
entityTypes.description: A scheduled community activity (voice hangout, Q&A, etc.)

entityTypes.name: Bot
entityTypes.description: An automated agent operating in the server

entityTypes.name: Message
entityTypes.description: A unit of communication posted in a channel

entityTypes.name: Role
entityTypes.description: A permission set + identity label assigned to members

entityTypes.name: Member
entityTypes.description: A person who has joined the server

entityTypes.name: Category
entityTypes.description: A grouping of channels

entityTypes.name: Channel
entityTypes.description: A space for communication (text, voice, forum, announcement)

entityTypes.name: Guild
entityTypes.description: Top level entity for a Discord Server

relationships.name: hosted in
relationships.description: Many events can be hosted in a channel over time; each event instance is hosted in one channel

relationships.name: attends
relationships.description: A member can attend many events; an event can have many attendees

relationships.name: targets
relationships.description: A member can receive many moderation actions over time; each action targets one member

relationships.name: assigns
relationships.description: A bot can assign many roles; a role can be assigned by many bots

relationships.name: enforces
relationships.description: One bot issues many moderation actions, but each action is issued by one source (bot or member)

relationships.name: monitors
relationships.description: A bot can monitor many channels; a channel can be monitored by many bots

relationships.name: spawns
relationships.description: One message can spawn one thread, each thread originates from one message

relationships.name: receives
relationships.description: One message can have many reactions, each reaction belongs to one message

relationships.name: appears in
relationships.description: Many messages live in a channel, each message belongs to one channel

relationships.name: posts
relationships.description: One member authors many messages, but each message has one author

relationships.name: grants access to
relationships.description: A role can unlock many channels; a channel can be accessible by many roles

relationships.name: holds
relationships.description: A member can hold multiple roles; a role can be held by many members

relationships.name: belongs to
relationships.description: A member can be in multiple servers; a server has many members

relationships.name: groups
relationships.description: One category holds many channels, but a channel belongs to one category (or none)

relationships.name: contains
relationships.description: One server has many channels, each channel belongs to one server

relationships.name: contains
relationships.description: One server has many categories, each category belongs to one server

ontology.name: Baby Routine Tracking
ontology.description: Models a baby’s daily routine by capturing caregiver-logged activities (sleep, feeding, diaper changes), associated observations, and emerging patterns to enable structured tracking and insights into the baby’s behavior.

entityTypes.name: DiaperChange
entityTypes.description: Represents a diaper change event, recording the type and condition of the diaper.

entityTypes.name: feeding
entityTypes.description: Represents a feeding event, including details such as type of feed (milk/solid) and quantity consumed.

entityTypes.name: sleep
entityTypes.description: Represents a sleep event, capturing the duration and timing of the baby’s sleep

entityTypes.name: Routine_Pattern
entityTypes.description: Represents a routine pattern summary that can be derived from the observations

entityTypes.name: Baby
entityTypes.description: Represents the infant whose daily routine activities (sleep, feeding, diaper changes) are being tracked and analyzed.

entityTypes.name: Care_Giver
entityTypes.description: Represents the individual (parent or guardian) responsible for logging activities and monitoring the baby’s routine.

entityTypes.name: Observation
entityTypes.description: Represents qualitative insights recorded during or after an activity, such as the baby’s mood (happy, irritated, stimulated etc) or other behaviors.

entityTypes.name: Activity
entityTypes.description: Represents any routine event performed for the baby, serving as the central entity for tracking sleep, feeding, and diaper-related activities.

relationships.name: is_an
relationships.description: Sleep is a sub-activity

relationships.name: is_an
relationships.description: Diaper Change is a sub-activity

relationships.name: is_a
relationships.description: Feeding is a sub-activity

relationships.name: derived_from
relationships.description: Routine can be derived from the activities that are logged

relationships.name: monitors
relationships.description: Care giver monitors the infant

relationships.name: has_observation
relationships.description: Each activity has certain observations

relationships.name: performed_for
relationships.description: Activities (eg diaper change, feeding, putting to sleep) are performed for baby

relationships.name: logs
